response:
CLOCK CLOCK CLOCK
THE FINAL WEIGHT OF THE FRAME IS CALCULATED AS W MULTIPLIED BY (0.98) RAISED TO THE POWER OF N
TO ACHIEVE A 25% REDUCTION IN ENERGY EXPENDITURE WE SOLVE THE EQUATION 0.25 EQUALS 1 MINUS (0.98) RAISED TO THE POWER OF N
SOLVING FOR N REQUIRES TAKING THE NATURAL LOGARITHM OF BOTH SIDES OF THE EQUATION
THE CALCULATION SHOWS THAT N MUST BE AT LEAST 13 TO MEET THE GOAL
THE CLOCK TICKS AS WE APPROACH THE SOLUTION
EVERY COMPONENT ADDED REDUCES THE WEIGHT AND IMPROVES EFFICIENCY
THE CLOCK SHOWS THE TIME IS RIGHT FOR THIS DECISION
ALEX SHOULD ADD THIRTEEN ADVANCED ITEMS TO THE BIKE
THE CLOCK REMINDS US THAT PRECISION MATTERS IN CYCLING
